member.name: T:NetSync.Diagnostics.INetLogger
member.summary: Logging abstraction so the core stays platform-agnostic. Implementations must be thread-safe: transports log from background threads.

member.name: T:NetSync.Diagnostics.NullNetLogger
member.summary: Discards everything. Default when no logger is supplied.

member.name: T:NetSync.Diagnostics.ConsoleNetLogger
member.summary: Writes to . Handy for samples and server apps.

member.name: T:NetSync.Diagnostics.NetMetrics
member.summary: Per-connection counters. All writes go through so the metrics can be read from any thread while network threads update them.

member.name: T:NetSync.Discovery.DiscoveryMessage
member.summary: LAN discovery datagram: [magic "NSD1":4][serverPort:2 BE][appIdLen:1][appId utf8]. The appId keeps unrelated NetSync applications on the same network apart.

member.name: T:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerBroadcaster
member.summary: Announces a server on the local network: periodically broadcasts a discovery datagram on every IPv4 LAN interface (Ethernet/Wi-Fi). The Unity-free replacement for the 1.x LocalNetworkBroadcaster.

member.name: F:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerBroadcaster.DefaultDiscoveryPort
member.summary: Default UDP port the discovery datagrams are sent to.

member.name: P:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerBroadcaster.TargetOverride
member.summary: Test hook: when set, datagrams go only to this endpoint instead of broadcast.

member.name: M:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerBroadcaster.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,NetSync.Diagnostics.INetLogger)
member.param: Application identity; listeners only react to a matching id.
The port clients should connect to.
UDP port listeners are bound to.

member.name: T:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerListener
member.summary: Listens for announcements and raises with the server's connectable endpoint. The event fires on a background thread and keeps firing for every announcement received — stop the listener once connected.

member.name: P:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerListener.Port
member.summary: Actual bound port (useful when constructed with port 0 in tests).

member.name: E:NetSync.Discovery.LanServerListener.ServerFound
member.summary: A matching server announced itself at this endpoint.

member.name: T:NetSync.Internal.NetTime
member.summary: Monotonic clock. DateTime.UtcNow is not monotonic (NTP jumps, DST issues on some platforms) and must not be used for RTT or timeout measurement.

member.name: P:NetSync.Internal.NetTime.NowMs
member.summary: Milliseconds since an arbitrary fixed origin. Monotonic, thread-safe.

member.name: F:NetSync.EventDelivery.Polled
member.summary: Events accumulate in a queue and fire inside PollEvents() on the caller's thread. The right choice for game loops (call PollEvents from Update).

member.name: F:NetSync.EventDelivery.Immediate
member.summary: Events fire directly on network threads for minimal latency. Handlers must be thread-safe and fast.

member.name: F:NetSync.KeyExchangeMode.None
member.summary: No encryption keys; channels with Encryption=true are rejected.

member.name: F:NetSync.KeyExchangeMode.PresharedKey
member.summary: Both sides share a secret key out-of-band; per-connection keys are derived from it and the session token. Simple and works everywhere.

member.name: F:NetSync.KeyExchangeMode.Ecdh
member.summary: Ephemeral ECDH (P-256) during the handshake: no pre-shared secret needed. Note: the exchange is not authenticated — it protects against passive eavesdropping, not an active man-in-the-middle.

member.name: T:NetSync.EncryptionConfig
member.summary: How per-connection encryption keys are established.

member.name: P:NetSync.EncryptionConfig.PresharedKey
member.summary: Shared secret for ; 32 bytes recommended, 16 minimum.

member.name: T:NetSync.ChannelConfig
member.summary: A logical channel: which transport carries it and with what guarantees.

member.name: P:NetSync.ChannelConfig.Reliability
member.summary: Delivery guarantees. Only applies to UDP channels — TCP is inherently reliable-ordered and ignores this setting. Reliable modes also enable automatic fragmentation, lifting the single-datagram size limit.

member.name: P:NetSync.ChannelConfig.Compression
member.summary: Deflate-compress payloads on this channel. Small payloads and payloads that do not shrink are sent uncompressed automatically (flagged per packet).

member.name: P:NetSync.ChannelConfig.Encryption
member.summary: Encrypt payloads on this channel (AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256, encrypt-then-MAC — the same cipher on every target so netstandard2.1/Unity and net8.0 peers interoperate). Requires mode ≠ None.

member.name: T:NetSync.NetConfig
member.summary: Shared configuration for and . Client and server must agree on the channel table (same ids, same transports). When is left empty, two defaults are used: channel 0 = TCP, channel 1 = UDP.

member.name: P:NetSync.NetConfig.PingIntervalMs
member.summary: Keepalive ping period per transport link.

member.name: P:NetSync.NetConfig.PingTimeoutMs
member.summary: Link is considered dead after this long without a pong; 0 disables.

member.name: P:NetSync.NetConfig.ConnectTimeoutMs
member.summary: Overall budget for ConnectAsync including the NetSync handshake.

member.name: P:NetSync.NetConfig.Encryption
member.summary: Key exchange for channels with Encryption enabled.

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.ChannelTransform
member.summary:
Per-channel payload pipeline: compress, then encrypt (compressing ciphertext is pointless). Runs before reliability/fragmentation, which treat the result as opaque bytes. Channels with neither feature skip this entirely — the extra flags byte only exists on channels that opted in.
Transformed layout: [flags:1][body]

member.name: F:NetSync.Peers.ChannelTransform.CompressionThreshold
member.summary: Payloads smaller than this are never worth deflating.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.ChannelTransform.Decode(NetSync.ChannelConfig,NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto,System.Byte[],NetSync.Diagnostics.INetLogger)
member.summary: Returns null when the packet must be dropped (tampered, malformed, downgraded).

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.EventDispatcher
member.summary: Routes events either straight through (Immediate) or into a queue drained by PollEvents (Polled). Handler exceptions are logged, never propagated into network threads.

member.name: P:NetSync.Peers.EventDispatcher.IsImmediate
member.summary: True in Immediate mode: callers can invoke handlers directly and skip the closure allocation that Dispatch would need.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.EventDispatcher.Poll(System.Int32)
member.summary: Drains queued events on the calling thread. No-op in Immediate mode.

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.NetClient
member.summary:
High-level client: connects all configured transports to one server in parallel and exposes them as channels. A random session token sent in the Hello message on every link lets the server group the links into a single logical connection.
Threading: with EventDelivery.Polled (default) call regularly; with Immediate, handlers run on network threads.

member.name: P:NetSync.Peers.NetClient.ConnectionId
member.summary: Server-assigned connection id, 0 before the handshake completes.

member.name: P:NetSync.Peers.NetClient.PingMs
member.summary: Lowest RTT across connected links, -1 when unknown.

member.name: E:NetSync.Peers.NetClient.DataReceived
member.summary: Application data: (channel, payload).

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetClient.PollEvents(System.Int32)
member.summary: Drains queued events (Polled mode). Returns the number of events handled.

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.NetConnection
member.summary: A connected client as seen by : one logical connection spanning one transport link per configured transport type.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetConnection.GetEndPoint(NetSync.TransportType)
member.summary: Endpoint of the link on the given transport, null when not bound.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetConnection.GetPing(NetSync.TransportType)
member.summary: RTT on the given transport link, -1 when unknown.

member.name: P:NetSync.Peers.NetConnection.PingMs
member.summary: Lowest RTT across bound links, -1 when unknown.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetConnection.DisconnectAsync
member.summary: Kicks this client: sends Bye on every link, then closes the connection.

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.NetServer
member.summary:
High-level server: runs one server transport per transport type used by the channel table, groups links arriving with the same session token into logical s and routes channel data.
A connection is announced via only once links for ALL configured transports completed the handshake; clients must therefore use the same channel table as the server.

member.name: E:NetSync.Peers.NetServer.DataReceived
member.summary: Application data: (connection, channel, payload).

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetServer.StartAsync(System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts listening on for every configured transport. Pass 0 to let the OS pick one port shared by all transports. Returns the bound port.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetServer.TryEstablishCrypto(NetSync.Peers.NetConnection,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Derives per-connection keys on first Hello. Returns false when the Hello is unusable.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetServer.BroadcastAsync(System.Byte,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends to every open connection; individual failures are logged, not thrown.

member.name: M:NetSync.Peers.NetServer.PollEvents(System.Int32)
member.summary: Drains queued events (Polled mode). Returns the number of events handled.

member.name: T:NetSync.Peers.PeerProtocol
member.summary:
Peer-level messages, carried inside transport Data payloads.
Hello [0x01][version:1][sessionToken:8][cipher:1][pubKeyLen:1][pubKey…] — client introduces itself on every link; the token groups multiple transport links into one logical connection. The cipher/pubkey tail is optional (absent = no encryption). Welcome [0x02][connectionId:8][pubKeyLen:1][pubKey…] — server accepted the link; the pubkey tail is present in ECDH mode. Reject [0x03][reason:1] — server refused. Bye [0x04][reason:1] — graceful disconnect notice. Data [0x10][channel:1][payload…] — application data.

member.name: F:NetSync.Peers.PeerProtocol.MsgRelData
member.summary: Reliability-layer data: [0x20][flags:1][channel:1][seq:2 BE][payload…]

member.name: F:NetSync.Peers.PeerProtocol.MsgRelAck
member.summary: Reliability-layer ack: [0x21][channel:1][anchorSeq:2 BE][mask:4 BE]

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Compression.Compressor
member.summary: Deflate payload compression. Format: [originalLength:4 BE][deflate stream]. The explicit length lets the receiver allocate exactly once and enforce a decompression-bomb cap.

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Compression.Compressor.MaxDecompressedBytes
member.summary: Refuse to inflate anything claiming to be larger than this.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Compression.Compressor.Decompress(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},NetSync.Diagnostics.INetLogger)
member.summary: Returns null when the blob is malformed, oversized or truncated.

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.FragmentBuffer
member.summary: Reassembles fragmented packets. Improvements over the 1.x version: fragments live in a flat array instead of a Dictionary, completeness is an O(1) counter check, stale-buffer cleanup is throttled instead of running on every fragment, and total buffered memory is capped.

member.name: P:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.FragmentBuffer.FragmentTimeoutMs
member.summary: Incomplete packets are dropped after this long without new fragments.

member.name: P:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.FragmentBuffer.MaxConcurrentPackets
member.summary: Upper bound on simultaneously reassembling packets; oldest is evicted.

member.name: P:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.FragmentBuffer.MaxFragmentsPerPacket
member.summary: Sanity cap: reject absurd fragment counts (protocol error / attack).

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.FragmentBuffer.AddFragment(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Adds a fragment. Returns the fully reassembled payload when this fragment completes the packet, otherwise null.

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter
member.summary: Splits large payloads into fragments and back. Fragment wire format: [Fragment:1][seqId:4 BE][index:4 BE][total:4 BE][data]

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter.FragmentSizeUdp
member.summary: Safe UDP fragment payload size: fits a typical MTU with headers to spare.

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter.FragmentSizeTcp
member.summary: TCP fragment payload size: keeps big transfers from starving small packets.

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter.MaxUnfragmentedSize
member.summary: Payloads at or above this size get fragmented.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter.WriteFragmentHeader(System.Span{System.Byte},System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Writes the 13-byte fragment header into (no allocation).

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Fragmentation.PacketFragmenter.TryUnwrap(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.UInt32@,System.UInt32@,System.UInt32@,System.Byte[]@)
member.summary: Returns true when is a fragment; outputs its header fields and payload. Payload is a copy (fragments outlive the receive buffer).

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint
member.summary:
Reliability engine for one unreliable datagram link (ENet-style ARQ). One instance per UDP link; transport-agnostic — it only needs a send delegate.
Per channel, depending on : - sequence numbers (16-bit, wrap-aware) with a 256-packet send window, - immediate ACKs carrying a 32-packet history bitmask, - RTO-based retransmission with exponential backoff, RTO derived from link RTT, - duplicate elimination and (for ReliableOrdered) reorder buffering, - automatic fragmentation of payloads above .
Threading: HandleRelData/HandleAck are called from the transport receive thread (one per link); Send* may be called from any thread; the retransmit loop runs on its own task. Sender state is guarded by a per-channel lock, receiver state is only touched by the receive thread.

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.FragmentThreshold
member.summary: Payloads above this size are split into reliable fragments (fits UDP MTU with headers).

member.name: F:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.SenderState.LowestUnacked
member.summary: Oldest unacked seq; equals NextSeq when nothing is pending. The send window is a RANGE gate: NextSeq may never run more than WindowSize ahead of this, otherwise the receiver's dedup ring (also WindowSize) could not distinguish new packets from ancient ones.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.HandleRelData(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Handles an incoming MsgRelData. Buffer valid only during the call.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.BuildAckMask(NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.ReceiverState,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Bit i set = seq (anchor - 1 - i) has been received.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.HandleAck(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Handles an incoming MsgRelAck. Buffer valid only during the call.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.AdvanceLowestUnacked(NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.SenderState)
member.summary: Recomputes LowestUnacked after acks. Returns true when it advanced. Caller holds the lock.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Reliability.ReliableEndpoint.SeqDistance(System.UInt16,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Wrap-aware distance: positive when is newer than .

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto
member.summary:
Per-connection symmetric cipher: AES-256-CBC with HMAC-SHA256, encrypt-then-MAC. Deliberately the same construction on every target framework so a netstandard2.1 (Unity) peer talks to a net8.0 peer over the wire (AesGcm is unavailable on netstandard2.1).
Message layout: [iv:16][mac:32][ciphertext:n×16], MAC over iv‖ciphertext.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto.FromSecret(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Derives independent encryption and MAC keys from a session secret.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto.FromPresharedKey(System.Byte[],System.Int64)
member.summary: PSK mode: session secret = HMAC(psk, label ‖ sessionToken). The token is public, the PSK is not — an eavesdropper cannot derive the session keys.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto.Decrypt(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Returns null when the message is malformed or fails authentication.

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.ConnectionCrypto.ComputeMac(System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: MAC over iv ‖ ciphertext of a [iv][mac][ciphertext] message.

member.name: T:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.EcdhKeyExchange
member.summary: Ephemeral ECDH (NIST P-256) helpers. Public keys travel as raw 64-byte X‖Y points — no ASN.1, works on both target frameworks. The exchange is unauthenticated: it defeats passive eavesdropping, not an active MITM (documented in ).

member.name: M:NetSync.Pipeline.Security.EcdhKeyExchange.DeriveSessionSecret(System.Security.Cryptography.ECDiffieHellman,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int64)
member.summary: Derives the session secret from our keypair, their public key and the session token.

member.name: T:NetSync.ReliabilityMode
member.summary: Delivery guarantees for a channel. Only meaningful on UDP: TCP channels are inherently reliable-ordered and ignore this setting.

member.name: F:NetSync.ReliabilityMode.Unreliable
member.summary: Fire-and-forget datagrams. Lowest latency, no guarantees.

member.name: F:NetSync.ReliabilityMode.UnreliableSequenced
member.summary: No retransmission, but stale packets are dropped: a packet older than the newest already delivered is discarded. Ideal for state snapshots (positions) where only the latest value matters.

member.name: F:NetSync.ReliabilityMode.Reliable
member.summary: Every packet arrives exactly once (ACK + retransmission), but order is not guaranteed. Large payloads are fragmented automatically.

member.name: F:NetSync.ReliabilityMode.ReliableOrdered
member.summary: Every packet arrives exactly once and in send order. Large payloads are fragmented automatically. TCP semantics over UDP.

member.name: F:NetSync.Sending.SendPriority.Critical
member.summary: Keepalive/control traffic. Always jumps the queue.

member.name: F:NetSync.Sending.SendPriority.Low
member.summary: Bulk transfers (file fragments) — never starve real-time traffic.

member.name: T:NetSync.Sending.SendQueue
member.summary:
Priority send queue with a dedicated drain loop.
Blocks on a counting semaphore and wakes exactly when work arrives (the 1.x version polled with Task.Delay(1): ~1 ms extra latency plus constant CPU wakeups).
Jobs are structs carrying (buffer, length); buffers rented from ArrayPool are returned after the write, so a steady send workload allocates nothing here.

member.name: M:NetSync.Sending.SendQueue.TryEnqueue(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Boolean,NetSync.Sending.SendPriority)
member.summary: Returns false when the queue is full (caller decides how to react and owns the buffer in that case). On success the queue takes ownership: pooled buffers are returned to the pool after the write.

member.name: T:NetSync.Serialization.NetReader
member.summary: Counterpart of : reads primitives from a received payload. Throws on malformed/truncated input — always wrap parsing of untrusted data in a try/catch.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetReader.ReadBytesMemory
member.summary: Zero-copy variant: a slice of the underlying payload.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetReader.ReadRaw(System.Int32)
member.summary: Raw bytes without a length prefix.

member.name: T:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter
member.summary: Compact binary writer for building message payloads — the replacement for the 1.x string-based JsonMessage. Little-endian fixed-width primitives, LEB128 var-ints, length-prefixed UTF-8 strings. Reusable: call and write the next message into the same buffer.

member.name: P:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WrittenMemory
member.summary: The written bytes; valid until the next write or Reset.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WriteVarUInt(System.UInt32)
member.summary: LEB128: small values take 1 byte instead of 4.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WriteVarInt(System.Int32)
member.summary: ZigZag + LEB128: small magnitudes (positive or negative) take 1 byte.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WriteString(System.String)
member.summary: UTF-8, var-int length prefix. Null is written as empty.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WriteBytes(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Var-int length prefix + raw bytes.

member.name: M:NetSync.Serialization.NetWriter.WriteRaw(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Raw bytes, no length prefix — the reader must know the size.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.DisconnectReason
member.summary: Why a connection ended. Replaces the old OnDisconnected/OnServerDisconnected event pair: subscribers get a single event with an explicit reason.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.DisconnectReason.Local
member.summary: Local side called Disconnect() or disposed the transport.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.DisconnectReason.Remote
member.summary: Remote side closed the connection.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.DisconnectReason.Timeout
member.summary: No pong received within the configured timeout.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.DisconnectReason.Error
member.summary: An unrecoverable transport error occurred.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.IServerTransport
member.summary: Server-side transport: listens on a port and manages many peers. Events are raised on background network threads (see notes).

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.IServerTransport.Port
member.summary: Actual bound port (useful when started with port 0), 0 when stopped.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.IServerTransport.StartAsync(System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts listening. Pass port 0 to let the OS pick one. Returns the bound port.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.IServerTransport.SendAsync(NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Scatter-gather variant, see notes.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.ITransport
member.summary:
Client-side transport: a single link to a remote server.
Threading contract: all events are raised on background network threads. Never block inside a handler; hand data off to your own queue if you need main-thread delivery (the Unity adapter does exactly that).
Lifetime contract: a transport instance is reusable — after Disconnect() completes it may be connected again with ConnectAsync().

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.ITransport.PingMs
member.summary: Last measured round-trip time in milliseconds, -1 when unknown.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.ITransport.SendAsync(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Queues data for sending. Returns once the payload is accepted by the send queue (not once it hits the wire). Throws if the transport is not connected or the queue is full (backpressure).

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.ITransport.SendAsync(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Scatter-gather variant: sends prefix immediately followed by payload as one packet, without requiring the caller to concatenate them. Lets upper layers prepend their headers with zero intermediate allocations.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.PacketProtocol
member.summary:
Wire format helpers. UDP datagram: [type:1][payload] TCP frame: [type:1][length:4 BE][payload] (Ping/Pong: [type:1][timestamp:8 BE])
Frame builders write into buffers: the hot send path allocates nothing. Every Rent* result must go back via . Rented arrays are usually longer than requested — always pair them with the out length, never use buffer.Length.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.PacketProtocol.RentUdpDataPacket(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int32@)
member.summary: UDP: [Data][prefix][payload] in one rented buffer.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.PacketProtocol.RentTcpDataFrame(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int32@)
member.summary: TCP: [Data][length][prefix][payload] in one rented buffer.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.PacketProtocol.RentTcpFragmentFrame(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int32@)
member.summary: TCP frame whose body is a fragment: [Data][length][Fragment][seq][idx][total][chunk].

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.Tcp.TcpServerTransport
member.summary: Server-side TCP transport: accepts clients, frames packets, pings peers and drops the ones that stop answering. Send/receive paths use pooled buffers.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.Tcp.TcpServerTransport.PooledReceive
member.summary: Zero-copy delivery hook, see .

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.Tcp.TcpTransport
member.summary:
Client-side TCP transport. Frames packets, keeps the link alive with ping/pong, fragments large payloads so bulk data never blocks small real-time packets.
Hot paths are allocation-free: frames are built in ArrayPool buffers, queued as structs and returned to the pool after the socket write; receive uses pooled buffers plus a single exact-size copy for delivery.
Reusable: each ConnectAsync builds a fresh connection state.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.Tcp.TcpTransport.PooledReceive
member.summary: Zero-copy delivery hook for the peer layer: receives the pooled buffer, valid ONLY for the duration of the call. When set, is not raised and no per-packet array is allocated by the transport.

member.name: M:NetSync.Transports.Tcp.TcpTransport.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,NetSync.Diagnostics.INetLogger)
member.param: How often to ping the server.
Disconnect when no pong arrives for this long; 0 disables the check.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer
member.summary: A remote peer as seen by a server transport. Replaces the old TransportClient: no untyped 'object Client' — transport-specific state stays inside the transport.

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer.Id
member.summary: Process-unique peer id.

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer.PingMs
member.summary: Last measured round-trip time in milliseconds, -1 when unknown.

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer.TransportState
member.summary: Transport-specific state (e.g. the TcpClient). Internal on purpose.

member.name: P:NetSync.Transports.TransportPeer.PeerLayerState
member.summary: Set by the peer layer once the handshake binds this link to a NetConnection.

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.Udp.UdpServerTransport
member.summary:
Server-side UDP transport. Tracks peers by source endpoint, pings them actively and expires the silent ones.
Fixes over 1.x: the server now *sends* pings (before, a peer's LastPongTime was set once at creation and never updated, so with a timeout configured every UDP client would eventually be dropped as dead); liveness uses any received packet.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.Udp.UdpServerTransport.PooledReceive
member.summary: Zero-copy delivery hook, see .

member.name: T:NetSync.Transports.Udp.UdpTransport
member.summary:
Client-side UDP transport. Connectionless at the socket level; liveness is tracked via ping/pong. The reliability layer (stage 4) adds retransmission, ordering and fragmentation on top.
Payload limit: one datagram, ≤ bytes.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.Udp.UdpTransport.MaxDatagramPayload
member.summary: 65507 (max UDP payload) minus our 1-byte packet type header.

member.name: F:NetSync.Transports.Udp.UdpTransport.PooledReceive
member.summary: Zero-copy delivery hook, see .
